observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

1. Municipal Engineer and/or the IC for Infrastructure Assets, to strictly comply with the guidelines in COA Circular No. 2015-008 in conducting the physical inventory and costing on road network and/or infrastructure assets; and 

2. Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile its record with the  Report of the Local Road Network,  Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and  Quarterly Report of Government Programs, Projects and Activities prepared and maintained by the Municipal Engineering Office or with the Statement of Receipts and Disbursements, and to secure the proper and complete documentation to vouch for the transfer of assets to its end-user or to the barangay where the assets were located, like a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or a Deed of Donation before dropping the same in its record, to establish the proper accountability for the safekeeping and  maintenance of the said government infrastructure assets.

3. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Engineer and Treasurer/ General Services Officer (GSO) to conduct the inventory in accordance with the guidelines set in IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008 and prepare the Annexes A, C, D and E and to compute its corresponding depreciation as required by the circular.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and Treasurer/GSO:
	
4. Evaluate the temporary evacuation center for its fair value as of date and submit a report to the Municipal Accountant for proper recognition of impairment in the books of accounts. Thereafter, a proper disposal shall be made for this impaired building; and

5. Conduct assessment of the PPEs whether there is any indication that an asset may be impaired. If any such indication exists, the entity shall estimate the recoverable service amount of the asset and to recognized it immediately in the FS as required in IPSAS 21.

8. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the IC, moving forward, to conduct and prepare RPCPPE of all recorded and owned properties, and reconcile the same with the accountant’s and GSO records.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
	
9. Municipal Accountant to record the supplies and material procured as inventory upon receipt of the AIR and record expenses only upon receipt of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS)/Distribution Lists from the GSO; and
 
10. Attach the JEV in all liquidation reports prepared to prove its recording.

11. Inventory Committee /GSO shall prepare AIR and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.

12. GSO to prepare SSMI and submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to: 

13. Strictly monitor the project and when negative slippage is incurred during the contract duration, implement calibrated measures provided under Government Policy Procurement Board Circular No. 03-2019 dated 8 March 2019, entitled “Guidance on Contract Termination due to Fifteen Percent (15%) Negative Slippage by the Contractor in Infrastructure Projects; 
	
14. For projects with advances for mobilization, to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the computation of outstanding advances and with the Municipal Treasurer for the collection and/or forfeiture of irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, upon termination of the contract; and

15. For the post-qualification team to emphasize the timely accomplishment of the projects as planned to prevent negative slippage and possible termination of the contract.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer to:
		
16. Assess the building and its premises and come up with necessary action to resolve the defects on the National Child Development Center building as recommended by the representative form the source agency, repair the comfort room and secure the building premises for the children’s safety; and
	
17. Review the status of the organic fertilizer production project and fast track its completion and notify the accountant upon completion.

18. Notify in writing the contractor that the retention money shall be used to defray for the necessary repairs to be made.

19. The BAC to blacklist the contractor from future projects of the LGU.
20. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents before certifying the box B of the DV.

21. We recommended, and Management agreed to require the BAC, through the Secretariat, to make the necessary postings, for information purposes, of all stages of procurements that need to be disseminated to concerned individuals, agencies and the public as well for transparency of the municipality’s procurement process as enumerated above, on the PhilGEPS website, website of the PE (if available) and at any conspicuous places reserved for this purpose in the premises of the PE for period of at least seven calendar days (CDs) for public bidding and three CD for alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
	
22. The BAC thru the BAC Secretariat to submit the eligibility documents mentioned in Appendix J.

23. Municipal Accountant ensure that payments for the procurement of goods are complete will all supporting documents to include eligibility documents as prescribed in RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed to: 
	
24. Require the end-users/responsible officials to completely fill-out the information in the PR, PO and AIR such as the date prepared, fund source, purpose, serial number, date, mode of procurement used, delivery term, payment term, date conformed by the supplier date prepared, accepted and inspected, and properly mark the boxes in the acceptance and inspection section, to comply with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and guidelines prescribed in MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, to use the numbering pattern of PRs and POs required by the instruction in the manual; and

25. The signatories of the documents, and the BAC Secretariat, to review the documents before affixing their signatures and return the document to the end-user or to the concerned office should the pre-requisite field has not been properly filled out.

26. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to stop this practice of advance deliveries and consumption instead plan the expected fuel consumption for the quarter and conduct the proper procurement process ensuring that POs are perfected before its consumption. Moreover, the municipality may opt to utilize the direct retail purchase of Petroleum fuel, Oil and Lubricant products as provided in Annex “H” (10) of the RIRR of RA 9184 subject to the conditions provided therein.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:

27. The Municipal Budget Officer to stop allowing obligation request for payment of MOOE incurred in the prior year charged to the current appropriation; and

28. All end-users/implementing office to submit to the Municipal Budget Office and Accountant their requests for payments during the year prior to year-end and closing of books.

29. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC Committee, together with the Municipal Engineer Office and the Technical Working Group, to submit advance copies of all perfected contracts and POs together with its supporting documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001, within five days to the COA office for its timely review and evaluation thereof.

30. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the LFC to carefully project a more realistic revenue target based on historical data and other corroborating factors, like marketability, weather, or any other conditions, to come up with an attainable estimate and to ensure that planned PPAs are supported by available cash.

34. We recommended, and Management agreed that the GFPS thru the HRMO establish a training plan for CY 2024 to capacitate the GFPS members, and Officials and Employees of the LGU to acquire knowledge on gender mainstreaming, development, and utilization of GAD tools and apply the same for effective and efficient implementation of GAD programs.

35. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made and render timely audit and information to management on the results of the audit. Non-submission without justifiable cause on the part of the officers and employees concerned on the required documents and financial report automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed by law.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
	
36. Focal person for the supplemental feeding program to submit the duly signed MOA and project proposal, distribution sheet containing information on the description of the items, the quantity of goods distributed, and a geotagged picture to prove its implementation.

37. BAC, to include in the invitation to bid and in the contract, information on the mode of delivery and payment period especially for food supplies and perishable goods. 

38. Accountant to ensure that a distribution sheet and geotagged pictures were attached to the SSMI, before purchased foods supplies for distribution be recorded to expenses.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the adjusting entries to recognize the erroneous recording of PPE using the following entries:

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
To turn-over the cost of structures to the barangay duly supported by certificate of turn-over and copy of JEV from the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the copy of the certificate of turn-over to the barangay:
Draw a JEV to derecognize the costs of the structures from the books of the LGU; and
Draw a JEV to recognize the book value of the structures in the books of the barangay.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed, to the following:
The HRMO, during the preparation of the Annual Budget, should ensure that the computation of terminal leave benefits includes not only retirable and co-terminus employees, as well as elected officials whose terms end during the current Annual Budget period, but also those employees and officials who are expected to retire or whose terms of service will end in the ensuing year. This will ensure that the appropriate terminal leave benefits are accurately accounted for and allocated in the budget;
The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget for the current year, ensuring that such allocations are made; and
The Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable, including those expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period supported by certification on the availability of appropriation and cash from the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Prior Period Adjustments			P4,036,990.20
Leave Benefits Payable				P4,036,990.20
To recognize prior years’ accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase in salary in CY 2025

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant draw a JEV to adjust the erroneous entry by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting TL-LDRRMF -CY 2024 in the amount of P2.428 million to recognize the liabilities brought about by the transfer of CY 2024 unexpended and unobligated QRF and MOOE.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the PPEs from Office Equipment to ICT Equipment in the amount of P3.253 million including the corresponding accumulated depreciation by debiting Accumulated Depreciation – Office Equipment and crediting Accumulated Depreciation – ICT Equipment in the amount of P1.932 million.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly review the transactions before posting in the books of account in accordance with IPSAS 1, PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose in the Notes to FS the nature and amounts of errors per line item of the FS affected, and other relevant information regarding the Prior Period Errors as required by Paragraph 54 of the IPSAS No. 3 to ensure the reliability and relevance of the FS.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the set up of the liabilities in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria set out in IPSASs.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the LDRRMF balances disclosed in the Notes to FS to include only the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF treated as STF and exclude amounts that have already been utilized in the previous years.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed that the LCE reconstitute the IC and relieve the designated members of IC from their regular tasks to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory count in accordance with the procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The LCE agreed to constitute the Disposal Committee tasked with implementing the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA-DBM JC No. 2024-1;
The IC to complete the conduct of a physical inventory of PPE and prepare the RPCPPE; and
The Property Division to prepare the IIRUP based on the RPCPPE, using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete, and/or no longer needed, and submit to the Disposal Committee for appraisal and disposal.

Recommendations
Moving forward, we recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed that:
Regularly prepare the BRS for all accounts on a timely basis to maintain a clear and accurate record of their financial status for transparency and accountability purposes; and
In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, undertake the necessary procedures for the closure of accounts that have been determined to be active but non-moving.

6. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment to recognize the understatement and pay the contractor upon request.
7. Moving forward, the Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices validate and reconcile with the bank, all the documents supporting every transaction before affixing signature to the box B of the DVs and preparation of JEVs.

39. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office to timely determine the unobligated and unexpended balance of current year DRRMF and prepare disbursement voucher; and 

40. Municipal Treasurer to draw a check to effect the transfer from the GF to TF books at year-end

41. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer or focal person to fast track the implementation of its program, projects and activities as planned, to deliver timely government services and benefits to its constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and disclose in the Notes to FS the following:

42. Lapsing Schedule for the PPE with emphasis on the movement of the asset, or reconciliation of the carrying amount at the beginning and end of the period, as required in IPSAS 17;

43. The SCBAA for which it is held publicly accountable and actual amounts, per IPSAS 24 guidelines and illustrative examples; and

44. Reconciliation of the SCBAA and Statement of Financial Performance as per Section 3.20 of Annex G, COA Circular No. 2016-004, moving forward.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a.	Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entry to record the land in the books at its fair market value as shown in the Tax Declaration; and 

b.	Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to account all future assets acquired through a non-exchange transaction at its acquisition costs or fair market value as at the date of acquisition, whichever is applicable.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a.	Municipal Accountant to transfer/reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts and record the accumulated depreciation from the time of its completion upon receipt of complete documents from the Municipal Engineer; and

b.	Municipal Engineering Office furnished the Municipal Accountant the certificate of completion and/or proof of completion as their basis for recording the completed assets in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
 
a.	The Municipal Engineer and concerned officer to fast track the implementation of pending/delayed/unimplemented PPAs and coordinate with the source agency on their disposition pertaining to those projects; 

b.	The Municipal Accountant prepare aging schedules and regularly monitor reconcile/confirm the balances with the source agency records and prepare adjustments if necessary; and

c.	Review the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), for guidance and proper disposition of the transferred funds and evaluate transactions with its corresponding source documents relating to long overdue payable accounts and the reason for non-payment and settle it accordingly.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to:

a.	Return the P2.161 million unexpended LDRRMF – CO to the GF that has been previously transferred to TF and be added back to the continuing appropriation in the SAAOB of the GF; and 

b.	Refrain from transferring to the TF-DRRMF the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CO instead be made continuing until fully utilized unless reverted/realigned/reprogrammed for new LDRRMF PPAs, moving forward.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

a.	Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to evaluate all stale checks and prepare all adjustments and replacements if necessary and investigate all unidentified reconciling items so that it can be adjusted/accounted in the books at the time of its discovery;

b.	Municipal Treasurer to regularly send a written notice, at least one month before the check becomes stale, to the payee of the existence of the check prospectively; and

c.	Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEV to adjust the inexistent balance of P673.11 in the CIB-LCCA of the TF of the LGU which is no longer existing per bank records as the amount was applied to charges for being dormant, and set up Other Receivable accounts - Due from Officers and Employees of the accountable officer concerned.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and make adjusting entries if necessary and to conduct regular reconciliation of their records, prospectively.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and disclose in the Notes to FS the following:

a.	Lapsing Schedule for the PPE with emphasis on the movement of the asset, or reconciliation of the carrying amount at the beginning and end of the period, as required in IPSAS 17;

b.	The SCBAA for which it is held publicly accountable and actual amounts, per IPSAS 24 guidelines and illustrative examples; and

c.	Reconciliation of the SCBAA and Statement of Financial Performance as per Section 3.20 of Annex G, COA Circular 2016-004, moving forward.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

a.	Municipal Treasurer to monitor the balances of all accountable forms and ensure that checkbooks are sufficient to support the agency’s operation at all times. Also, refrain from transferring funds from GF to TF to defray administrative government expenditures; and

b.	Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the rules and regulations in processing and recording government transactions and improved internal control as regards to their accountable forms.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MDRRMO to:

a.	Prepare the LDRRMFIP with clear segregation of PPAs to be funded from the current year appropriation and PPAs funded from TF; and

b.	The PPAs in the LDRRMFIP be specifically identified instead of a generic term to establish connectivity of data per PPAs from planning to implementation for proper monitoring and evaluation of the effectiveness and efficiency of their plan and its utilization.
The PIF was not prepared and submitted on or before April 30 of each year for purposes of determining all the insurable properties of the Municipality in violation of COA Circular 2018-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law; conduct information and education campaign on RPT payment; initiate measures to increase the collection as allowed under the LGC; and reconcile its records with the records of the Municipal Assessor’s Office and update all taxpayer ledger cards, to come-up with the detailed and precise listing of delinquent taxpayers to be submitted to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office for collection remedies.
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to COA Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer to:

Submit justifications, duly supported with the necessary documents, for the delay in the completion of the projects.

observations.reason: (Partially Implemented)

observations.recommendation: The Solid Waste Management Board will fast track the procurement of the needed machines so they can operate the soonest possible time and prevent the plastic garbage to filled-up, instead turned it into a useful product and/or adapt a recycling scheme that would help the household to be more creative in their junks and transform it into a useful one.

Management to direct the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and a schedule for the status of the implementation of the specified projects, whether completed or not, and whether the counterpart was utilized or not, as the basis for the reclassification or adjusting entries to be made, as follows:

a. For portion of completed projects with remaining unutilized counterpart – return to GF by debiting Due from Other Funds in the Trust Fund (TF) book; and crediting to Prior Years Adjustment in the General Fund (GF) books; and

b. For uncompleted projects, counterpart be reclassified as credit to Trust Liability account (2-04-01-010) and upon completion of the project or at year-end whichever comes first, to be recognized as income by crediting Transfers for Project Equity Share/LGU Counterpart account (4-03-02-010) for its utilization and debit the same to Trust Liability (2-04-01-010).
observations.reason: Reiterated/Restated in Part II - AOM# 01

(Partially Implemented)

Reiterated/Restated in Part II- AOM# 08
Reiterated/Restated in Part II - AOM# 07

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact all collections daily as mandated. If it is not possible to do it due to the distance of the agency’s location to the AGDB, twice a week is tolerable, as long as the risk of keeping the collections will be considered and will not be compromised and it is still be done intact.
observations.reason: Implemented in CY 2022

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that Management  direct  the  Accounting  Office  to
       prepare the necessary adjusting entries and schedules for the status
       of the implementation of the specified projects,  whether  completed
       or not, and whether the counterpart was  utilized  or  not,  as  the
       basis for the reclassification or adjusting entries to be  made,  as
       follows:

       a. For the portion of completed projects with  remaining  unutilized
          counterpart, return to GF if there is an  intention  to  returned
          the  savings/unutilized  counterpart,  otherwise,  close  to  the
          appropriate income account using the above pro-forma entry; and

       b. For uncompleted projects, counterpart be reclassified  as  credit
          to Trust Liability account (2-04-01-010) and upon  completion  of
          the project or at year-end whichever comes first,  be  recognized
          as income by crediting Transfers  for  Project  Equity  Share/LGU
          Counterpart account (4-03-02-010) for its utilization  and  debit
          the same to Trust Liability (2-04-01-010).

11. We recommended that the Management to direct the:


       a. Municipal Accountant informed the preparer of the  Journal  Entry
          Voucher (JEV) to debit the appropriate expense or  asset  account
          upon incurrence of the expenditure and debit only  the  liability
          account (Due to NGAs and Due to Other LGUs) at  the  end  of  the
          year or upon the completion/fulfilment of the  condition  of  the
          transfer;


       b. Municipal Accountant to employ control  mechanism  such  as  peer
          review to lessen or eliminate the possible clerical error in  the
          future.

       c. To monitor, through the office of the Municipal Engineer together
          with   the   Municipal    Accountant,    the    compliance    and
          completion/fulfilment of the conditions in the Due to  NGAs,  Due
          to Other LGUs and Trust Liabilities accounts, and  the  later  to
          book up the corresponding income upon  the  completion/fulfilment
          of the condition of the transfer. Since, the affected account are
          nominal accounts, it must be dealt prospectively; and

       d. Municipal Engineer to document the proof of  transfer,  turn-over
          or  approved  donation   documents   for   those   Infrastructure
          Assets/PPE located and in the position of the  Barangay  and  the
          Municipal accountant to derecognized the said assets in the  book
          of the transferor (Municipality) and record the same in the  book
          of the transferee (Barangay) as follows;



       Municipality Book:


       DR: Subsidy to Other Local Government Units          42,421,231.35

       CR:                           Road                           Network
                           42,421,231.35
                 #
       Barangay Book:

       DR:                           Road                           Network
        42,421,231.35
       CR:    Subsidy     from     Other     Local     Government     Units
        42,421,231.35
                 #

6. We recommended that the Management, through the Accounting Office  to
      refrain from using inappropriate accounts and to utilize COA Circular
      No. 2015-009 dated 01 December 2015 as guide in  choosing  the  right
      account to be used.

4. We recommended  that  Management  direct  the  Accounting  Office  to
      prepare  the  necessary   adjusting   entry   for   the   understated
      depreciation expense of office equipment and to be extra  careful  in
      the account codes being used in the future, specially that the agency
      is employing a software program, where these are vital and  important
      input to come up with an accurate and correct accounts  presented  in
      the Financial Statement.

6. We recommended the management to apply the  capitalization  threshold
      of P15,000.00 of all purchases onwards  and  to  make  any  necessary
      adjustment to all PPEs below the  threshold  that  is  still  in  the
      records of the agency’s book.

23. In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:

       a. The Inventory Committee to assign a unique  Property  Number  for
          each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering
          system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular;



       b.   The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP and submit the same to
          the COA  Audit  Team  at  least  ten  calendar  days  before  the
          scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with
          the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular;



       c.   Each office of the LGU to fill up the ICF and submit  the  same
          to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used
          by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP
          and will be considered as part of their inventory working  paper.
          Moreover, the Inventory Committee to record/document the physical
          count daily using the ICF per PPE  sub-major  account  group  and
          will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical
          count;



       d.   The Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs  Found  at  Station
          (Annex B of the Circular) and List of  Non-Existing/Missing  PPEs
          (Annex C of the Circular) during physical  inventory  and  submit
          these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books  of
          accounts of  the  LGU  to  establish  the  existence  and  proper
          accountability of the PPE accounts;



       e.   The Property Unit to prepare the PCs for all the property owned
          by the Agency from prior years and onwards; and IIRUP  and  RDPPE
          for PPEs to be and already derecognized; and


       f. Strictly observe the guidelines and procedures in the conduct  of
          physical count of PPE as provided in  COA  Circular  2020-006  to
          facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

9. We recommended the management of the direct the:


       a. GSO  or  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  maintain  SCs  to  record
          purchases and issuances of inventories; and  prepare  and  submit
          SSMI to the Accounting Office to support the  dropping  from  the
          books of all issued/consumed supplies and materials, as  required
          in Sec. 119 and 121 of the MNGAS; and


       b.  Accounting  Office  to  record  all  purchases  of  supplies  as
          inventory upon  payment  and  record  the  corresponding  expense
          account upon issuance with SSMI and RIS as proof.   Likewise,  to
          maintain the SLCs per stock number, as required in  Sec.  120  of
          the MNGAS, and reconcile with  the  GSO’s  record  and  make  the
          necessary  adjustment  the  true  balances   in   the   financial
          statement.

7.  We  recommended  that  the  Management  to  instruct  the  Municipal
      Accountant to;

       a. Prepare the lapsing schedule and  incorporate  the  same  in  the
          notes to financial statement (FS) of the PPE and  reconcile  them
          with the PCs and PAR prepared  and  maintained  by  the  property
          unit/custodian; and


       b. Enhance the notes to FS pertaining to LDRRMF  and  segregate  the
          MOOE and CO for mitigation fund.  Mention  also  the  amount  and
          composition of the amount transferred to TF for the year.

9.10. We recommended that Management ensure that the accounting forms and
       reports  are  duly   filled   up   and   supported   with   complete
       documentation, to provide stronger internal  control  of  government
       operations, in accordance with the Volume II of the  New  Government
       Accounting System Manual for  LGUs  (LGU-MNGAS)  that  provides  the
       forms and instruction on how to fill them.

4. We recommended that the Management (a) submit a copy of  the  Annual
       GPB to the COA Audit Team within five working days from the  receipt
       of the approved plan and annual GAD Accomplishment  Report  complete
       with the required documents for proper evaluation thereof; the Local
       Chief Executive to direct the MPDO  to  maintain  GAD  Database  and
       establish a gender  issues  and  concerns  thru  conduct  of  gender
       analysis,  generation  and  review  of  sex-disaggregated  data  and
       consultations with gender advocacies and women  clientele  as  basis
       for the attribution of the LGU’s GAD Plan and  Budget  in  order  to
       fully assess  the  effectiveness  and  efficiency  of  their  gender
       mainstreaming efforts.

The Municipal Mayor to direct the Accounting Office to enforce strictly the control mechanism on the submission of the barangay financial transaction documents through the issuance of formal letters informing the Punong Barangays within the dates mandated, furnish copy of these letters the audit team and the immediate compliance of the duties and procedures specified in Item 4 – Specific Guidelines of COA Circular 2019-001 in order to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of barangay.

The management to direct the Accounting Unit:

To prepare necessary adjustments to correct the misstatements and to account subsequent mobilization fees as “Advances to Contractors”, subject to recoupment every progress payment the Revised IRR of RA 9184, COA Circular 2015-009 and the GAM for LGUs;

To prepare adjusting entries and recognize retention money as “Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable” and strictly follow the guidelines required under IRR of RA 9184 Annex E and GAM for LGU; and 

To recognize income in the trust fund upon the fulfillment of the condition of the grant to be closed to its corresponding Due to NGAs account. When the asset will be transferred to the General Fund it shall be closed to Government Equity account in accordance with GAM-LGU Volume I page 66-67.

observations.reason: Reiterated/ Restated in Part II -AOM# 06

observations.recommendation: Management to reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve (12) months from balance sheet date on all its long-term debts as Current Liabilities to subscribe to the provisions on recording of loan amortization, in accordance with the LGU Government Accounting Manual.

The Management through the BAC, to ensure that for all future undertakings:
a)	Prospectively, procurement through emergency cases must be posted in the GPPB Online Portal;

b)	All the necessary documentation required must be listed in the Note of the RFQ. Like in the case of Negotiated Procurement where there are additional documents required specially the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) which should specify the name of the project/procurement undertaken and must be in original copy; and

c)	To observe the procurement procedures for Negotiated Procurement through Emergency cases under Section 53.2, RIRR of RA NO. 9184 and ensure that the RFQ provides for the prescribed period for submission of documentary requirements by the suppliers and all the necessary information therein. Likewise, pre-procurement planning is necessary to carefully scrutinize all the needed specifications of the requisitioned items are included therein which would be useful in determining the price reasonableness and compliance with specifications.

We recommend that the management require the Accountable Officer to comply with the preparation and submission of the Report for Accountability of Accountable Forms (RAAF) on time, for local treasurer’s consolidation and monitor the movement of accountable forms at their possession to ensure reliability of the monthly balances of inventory and submission of the Consolidated Report for Accountability of Accountable Forms (CRAAF) to the Office of the Team Leader, in compliance Sections 50-51 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units.

observations.recommendation:
The Solid Waste Management Board to fast track the procurement of the needed machines so they can operate the soonest possible time and prevent the plastic garbage to filled-up, instead turned it into a useful product and/or adapt a recycling scheme that would help the household to be more creative in their junks and transform it into a useful one

Also, we reiterated, the Board together with the Municipal Information Officer to conduct an intensive information campaign on its solid waste management plan with emphasizes on mandatory segregation at source from households, commercial, institutional, market areas, industrial sources, transport facilities and utilities, agricultural areas and construction business, imposing the corresponding penalties for non-compliance and enforce the Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-06 on Solid Waste Management System to be imposed strictly with constant monitoring.

The local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer to be use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers with its corresponding amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then, the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at every beginning of the year for the total/aggregated amount of tax due and collectible for the RPT and SET.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal assessor would employ corrective measure for that multiple assessment issue so that they can generate a more realistic report of assessment.

The Municipal Accounting Office to properly classify and recognize their accounts in the financial statements in accordance with COA circular no. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 so as not to mislead the reader/user of the agency’s financial information.

The Municipal Mayor to direct the Accounting Office to enforce strictly the control mechanism on the submission of the barangay financial transaction documents through the issuance of formal letters informing the Punong Barangays within the dates mandated, furnish copy of these letters the audit team and the immediate compliance of the duties and procedures specified in Item 4 – Specific Guidelines of COA Circular 2019-001 in order to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of barangay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the local Chief Executive to require the Municipal assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayers’ assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer to be use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers with its corresponding amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then, the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at every beginning of the year for the total/aggregated amount of tax due and collectible for the RPT and SET.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal assessor would employ corrective measure for that multiple assessment issue so that they can generate a more realistic report of assessment.

We recommended that management direct:
The Accounting and the Treasurer’s Office coordinate with each other to secure the proper documentation of the two (2) donated ambulances worth P2.4 million from the PCSO and DOH for its proper recognition in the books of accounts.
The Municipal Treasurer the immediate renewal of all the LGU’s delinquent and invalid motor vehicle registrations in compliance with RA 4136 otherwise known as the Land Transportation and Traffic Code; and include the renewal of the insurance policies of these motor vehicles with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).

We recommended the Municipal Accounting Office to properly classify and recognize their accounts in the financial statements in accordance with COA circular no. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015 so as not to mislead the reader/user of the agency’s financial information.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accounting Office to enforce strictly the control mechanism on the submission of the barangay financial transaction documents through the issuance of formal letters informing the Punong Barangays within the dates mandated, furnish copy of these letters the audit team and the immediate compliance of the duties and procedures specified in Item 4 – Specific Guidelines of COA Circular 2019-001 in order to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of barangay.

We recommended that management require BAC officials concerned to comply with the submission of the Semestral Procurement Monitoring Report to enable a proper evaluation of the procurement activities of the Municipality, and to invite the Auditor as witness in all stages of procurement process three (3) days before the date of the activities.

We recommended that Management comply with the regulations on settlement of accounts by:
a) monitoring regularly the status of NSs and NDs;
b) enforcing the submission of required documents in the NS; and
c) enforcing settlement of the NDs without delay or requiring the Municipal Treasurer to deduct from the salary of the persons liable in the Notice of Disallowance.
d) draw up adjusting entries to correct the balance of the 1-03-06-010, Receivables – Disallowances/Changes account and submit the required documents for settlements for the issuance of NSSDC.

We recommended that the Municipality institute measures to intensify its collection capability to ensure achievement of its targeted revenue particularly on the rentals of heavy equipment. Moreover, revise its estimated revenue based on the past years’ actual data in order to establish an estimated revenue that is realizable. We further recommend that the offices concerned formulate specific guidelines for the Economic Enterprises (EE) in the use and rental of the heavy equipment and establish a control on the motor pool where the equipment are parked/stored.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive or Administrative Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team in consonance with the provisions of Item 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan formulate and adopt a Local GAD Code in consonance with Section 36a of the Republic Act No. 9710-Magna Carta of Women (MCW) as a requirement to ensure that the Philippine laws on women and gender equality are translated and fulfilled at the local level.

We recommend that Management:
Direct the concerned offices concerned with the preparation and utilization of LDRRMF Investment Plan to strictly adhere to the Programs, Projects and Activities allocation and the LGU’s Appropriation Law; and
Refrain from utilizing unexpended Quick Response Fund from the previous years without being reviewed and updated to the LDRRMF Investment Program annually to include the activities said periods in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002, The Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for LDRRMF of LGUs.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board will fast track the procurement of the needed machines so they can operate the soonest possible time and prevent the plastic garbage to filled-up, instead turned it into a useful product and/or adapt a recycling scheme that would help the household to be more creative in their junks and transform it into a useful one.

Also, we reiterated, the Board together with the Municipal Information Officer to conduct an intensive information campaign on its solid waste management plan with emphasizes on mandatory segregation at source from households, commercial, institutional, market areas, industrial sources, transport facilities and utilities, agricultural areas and construction business, imposing the corresponding penalties for non-compliance and enforce the Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-06 on Solid Waste Management System to be imposed strictly with constant monitoring.

We recommended that:
The Municipality submit an explanation or justification as to the reason why OHTAKE TRADING CO., LTD. was named the beneficiary in the LC instead of JVF Commercial and Project Development Support Services, the supplier in whose name the contract was awarded; and,
The Municipality refrain from opening an LC in favor of a Philippine entity’s foreign manufacturer or supplier where the Philippine entity acts as the distributor or local supplier of the foreign manufacturer or supplier, which directly participates in the bidding process and in whose name the contract is awarded which is contrary to Section 42.6 of the IRR of RA 9184 (as Amended) and GPPB Non-Policy Opinion NPM No. 051-2004.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality comply with the requirements of Section 42.6 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 to avoid the transaction from suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management comply with the regulations on settlement of accounts by:
monitoring regularly the status of NSs and NDs;
enforcing the submission of required documents in the NS; and
enforcing settlement of the NDs without delay or requiring the Municipal Treasurer to deduct from the salary of the persons liable in the Notice of Disallowance.
draw up adjusting entries to correct the balance of the 1-03-06-010, Receivables – Disallowances/Changes account and submit the required documents for settlements for the issuance of NSSDC.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to update the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements in consonance with Section 74 of PD 1445 so that reconciling items can be promptly identified and adjustments can be made as needed to come up with reliable Cash in Bank account balances.
We further recommended management direct the Accountant to prepare a JEV to record the staled checks as Accounts Payable and strictly observe the provisions of NGAS Manual for LGU.

We reiterated that management (a) create an inventory committee to conduct complete inventory count of all property, plant and equipment at least once a year in accordance with regulations and reconcile with the balances per accounting records; (b) require the Treasurer’s Office to update the AREs and maintain a complete file thereof; (c) require the Accounting Office to maintain Equipment Ledger Cards/subsidiary ledger cards; (d) investigate and make appropriate actions on discrepancies, if any.

We recommended that management direct the Property Officer to prepare and submit inventory of insurable physical assets, determine appraisal value and insure physical assets with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and include the premium in the Annual Budget of the agency.
We further recommended that management direct the concerned offices to reconcile their reports and properly maintain their records.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the members of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to ensure that the programs, projects and activities to be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) shall cover the priority LDRRMF projects and activities that are included or related to the DRRM activities as declared under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. Be guided with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01;
initiate the creation of the four (4) statutory and mandatory DRRM positions composed of one (1) Local DRRM Officer and three (3) DRRM staff positions as required under Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1; and
provide for the amount and details discussing the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the accountable officers turn over/deposit their collections daily/regularly to the liquidating officer or with the bank as required under Section 29 of NGAS, Volume I, to ensure the prevention of possible risk of loss and unauthorized use.

Further, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to monitor regularly all accountable officers to adhere existing policies on proper cash remittances and deposits of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LGU legislate an ordinance on Ecological Solid Waste Management System, incorporating the 10-year ESWM Plan, providing the necessary institutional mechanism and incentives, declaring prohibitions with the corresponding penalties thereon by the violators to be imposed strictly with constant monitoring.

Also, the Solid Waste Management Board together with the Municipal Information Officer conduct an intensive information campaign for mandatory segregation at source form households, commercial, institutional, market areas, industrial sources, transport facilities and utilities, agricultural areas and construction business, reminding the corresponding penalties for non-compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the  accountable  officers  turn  over/deposit
      their collections daily/regularly to the liquidating officer  or  with
      the bank as required under Section 29 of NGAS, Volume I, to ensure the
      prevention of possible risk of loss and unauthorized use.


           Further,  we  recommend  that  the  Office  of   the   Municipal
      Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal  Treasurer
      to monitor regularly  all  accountable  officers  to  adhere  existing
      policies on proper cash remittances and deposits of the Municipality.

We reiterate that management (a) create an  inventory  committee
      to conduct  complete  inventory  count  of  all  property,  plant  and
      equipment at least once a year  in  accordance  with  regulations  and
      reconcile with the balances per accounting records;  (b)  require  the
      Treasurer’s Office to update the AREs and  maintain  a  complete  file
      thereof;   (c)   investigate   and   make   appropriate   actions   on
      discrepancies, if any.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipality  institute  measures  to
      intensify its collection  capability  to  ensure  achievement  of  its
      targeted revenue particularly  on  the  rentals  of  heavy  equipment.
      Moreover, revise its estimated revenue based on the past years’ actual
      data in order to establish an estimated revenue that is realizable.

We recommend that the LGU remit promptly and intact the share of
      the provincial government to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office monthly
      within fifteen (15) days  of  the  succeeding  month  as  required  in
      Section 9 of Republic Act (RA) No. 5447,  for  timely  utilization  of
      fund to effectively serves it purpose.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal   Accountant   examine   the
      completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of  the
      Municipality before approving the same.


           Also, require  the  concerned  office  to  submit  the  complete
      supporting documents, otherwise, transactions  will  be  suspended  in
      audit.

We  recommend   that   the   Municipality   submit   a   written
      justification as to why the  project  was  not  completed  within  the
      contract time duly supported with variation  orders  and  contractor’s
      notice.
           Also, we recommend that the Office  of  the  Municipal  Engineer
      monitor regularly all  projects  implemented  by  the  LGU  to  ensure
      projects are completed without unexpected losses to the municipality.

We recommend that the Management  transfer  only  the  unexpended
     balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE excluding  those  intended  for  capital
     outlay, in the special trust account of the Trust Fund Books and ensure
     the preparation of annual LDRRMFIP and the inclusion of  the  necessary
     notes to financial statement of the fund status  as  per  COA  Circular
     2012-002.

We recommend that the LGU legislate an ordinance  on  Ecological
      Solid Waste Management System, incorporating the  10-year  ESWM  Plan,
      providing  the  necessary  institutional  mechanism  and   incentives,
      declaring prohibitions with the corresponding penalties thereon by the
      violators to be imposed strictly with constant monitoring.


           Also,  the  Solid  Waste  Management  Board  together  with  the
      Municipal  Information  Officer  conduct  an   intensive   information
      campaign  for  mandatory  segregation  at  source   form   households,
      commercial, institutional, market areas, industrial sources, transport
      facilities  and  utilities,  agricultural   areas   and   construction
      business, reminding the corresponding penalties for non-compliance.

We  recommend  the  Municipality  monitor  timely  and   optimal
      utilization of the 1% Local Council for  the  Protection  of  Children
      (LCPC) and cause the judicious implementation of programs and projects
      intended under the fund to  ensure  the  attainment  of  the  expected
      desirable outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the following Municipal Departments Heads to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015: A.  The Municipal Accountant 1. Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively; 2. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructure for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry; 3. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and 4. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: a total of P34,561,706.16 Road Networks was reported by the Municipal Accountant for CY 2017 Book of Accounts of which P25,691,288.07 was taken from the Prior years’ summary of Public Infrastructures. The said amount was already more than 50% as required by COA circular #2015-008 dated Nov. 23, 2015 in the 2nd year of implementa tion of the PPSAS.
observations.reason: Booked up more than 50% of Road Networks as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management fast track the implementation of 20% development fund to ensure that the appropriated funds will not be idle and hamper economic development. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management refrain from charging the regular expenses to the Special Education Fund. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management (a) create an inventory committee
       to conduct  complete  inventory  count  of  all  property,  plant  and
       equipment at least once a year  in  accordance  with  regulations  and
       reconcile with the balances per accounting records;  (b)  require  the
       Treasurer’s Office to update the AREs and  maintain  a  complete  file
       thereof; (c) require  the  Accounting  Office  to  maintain  Equipment
       Ledger  Cards/subsidiary  ledger  cards;  (d)  investigate  and   make
       appropriate actions on discrepancies, if any.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  following
       Municipal Departments Heads to comply with  the  requirements  of  COA
       Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

           A.  The Municipal Accountant

               1. Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record  the  beginning
                 balance of the local road network and its components in the
                 general ledger and  the  Local  Road  Network  Ledger  Card
                 (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;

               2. Support the JV with the Registry of Public  Infrastructure
                 for Roads, working paper on the distribution of  costs  for
                 the road components, and working paper on the determination
                 of the depreciated replacement  cost  for  road  components
                 with no available cost per registry;

               3. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for  roads  and  road
                 components for every road network; and

               4. Prepare a lapsing schedule  for  the  computation  of  the
                 depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

           B.  The Municipal General Services Officer

                  1.   Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

                  2.   Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for
                 all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

           C.  The Municipal Engineer

                  1.   Provide the Municipal Accountant and  the  Municipal
                 General Services Officer with the complete description  and
                 cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommend that the Management fast track  the  implementation
       of 20% development fund to ensure that the appropriated funds will not
       be idle and  hamper  economic  development.   Also,  the  Municipality
       should only consider programs/projects/activities that  are  qualified
       under DBM-DILG JMC No. 2011-01 to provide appropriate and  sustainable
       development programs and projects intended thereon.

We recommend that  the  management  refrain  from  charging  the
       regular expenses to the Special Education Fund. Instead,  allocate  or
       use such fund for the construction and  repair  of  school  buildings;
       facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition  of  books
       and periodicals;  and  sports  development  which  cannot  be  charged
       against the downloaded MOOE of  the  schools.  Also,  the  LSB  should
       evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included  in  the  SEF
       budget, taking into consideration the  objectives  set  forth  by  the
       provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  immediately  effect
       necessary adjustments to correct the excessive contributions  made  by
       the municipality in its employer’s share to all employees monthly Pag-
       IBIG premium contributions in accordance with  Section  7  of  RA  No.
       9679.

We recommend that the Municipality fast track the submission  of
       required documents to the National Government Agencies that  are  pre-
       requisite  to  the  release  of  funds  to  ensure  BuB  projects  are
       implemented on time.
           Also, we recommend that Municipality coordinate  regularly  with
       the DBM to ensure  funds  of  BuB  projects  and  other  programs  are
       released on time.

We recommended  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  LDRRM  Office  to
       strictly  adhere  with  the  provision  of  NDRRMC,  DBM,  DILG  Joint
       Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Section 12  of
       Republic Act (RA) 10121  -  Philippine  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
       Management Act of 2010 and to ensure  prompt  implementation  of  more
       projects/programs/activities  for  the  preparedness,  preventions   &
       mitigations of disaster risk.

We reiterate that  the  municipality  implement  the  Ecological
       Solid  Waste  Management  system  such  as  segregation   at   source,
       segregated  collection  and  establishment   of   Materials   Recovery
       Facility/s, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its
       jurisdiction.

We reiterate that the Municipality conduct the actual physical count on the inventories/property and submit the required reports. Also, we reiterate that all Offices/Departments of the Municipality conduct their respective physical count of supplies and/or property and prepare the required reports to be submitted to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer for consolidation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management partly and substantially complied with and give assurance for its full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend on the specifications for the signboards, billboards and tarpaulins that are to be posted at all venues where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit are to be implemented in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy of all transactions governing public interest. Moreover, to refrain from disciplinary action that may be instituted under the said COA Circular’s penalty clause.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is in the process of implementing this audit finding

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the responsible officials conduct gender analysis and assessment so that they can formulate policies and plans which can address gender issues in their LGU and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality. Furthermore, institute GAD Codes, which integrates or is aligned to gender-related national laws and international conventions.
observations.managementAction: Management intends to provide programs and projects for the priority need of it’s constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to cautiously monitor the utilization of the 20% development fund and cause the judicious implementation of development programs and projects with the end view of complying with the authoritative issuances appurtenant thereto and the attainment of expected desirable outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the preparation of PPMP & APP and submit the same on the time stipulated/required.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate that the Municipality implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of Materials Recovery Facility/s, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its jurisdiction.

We recommended that all end-users of the LGU shall prepare their respective PPMP for their different programs, activities and projects and to submit the same to the BAC for consolidation in the APP. Further, copies of the PPMP and APP should be furnished to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of each fiscal year.
